text: The surgeon enters the abdominal cavity at the wang point, located midway between the posterior abdomen and the anterior chest cavity.
The video shows a dense curtain of adhesions surrounding the intestines, obstructing the view of the uterus area.
The surgeon reveals the intestines, which are precisely covered by the stomach wall, after addressing the initial obstruction.
The surgeon encounters a mass covered by splinters on one side, obstructing the ability to connect or manipulate the mass without first disassembling the splinters from the abdominal wall.
The surgeon completes the dissection on the lower side and proceeds to address the connection of the mass with the side wall of the pelvic basin, lowering the mass from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon lowers a yellow tissue mass, which is being carefully manipulated and repositioned within the surgical field.
The surgeon continues to dismantle the adhesions, gradually separating the tissue connections until the masses and their boundaries become visually distinct.
The surgeon uses a protective screen to prevent damage to the intestines while carefully removing the rectum from the surrounding tissue or 'bag'.
The surgeon completes the extraction of the mass and uses a surgical screen to apply pressure, ensuring no residual liquid remains in the area.
The surgical field shows no residual liquid at the site of the previous injection, indicating successful fluid management during the procedure.
The surgeon places a plastic bag into the surgical field, preparing to use it with the screen for further procedures.
The surgeon places a Surgicil mesh into the surgical site, designed to prevent cleaning through chemical interaction.
The surgeon inserts a drain tube to release a blood clot, as referred to by the patient.
